MS Graduation

From Mumbai
to Chicago

I grew up in my family's electronics business in Mumbai, where supply chain wasn't a subject — it was whether the shipment showed up and whether we got paid. Chasing vendors, negotiating rates, fixing stockouts: that was daily life long before I had textbook words for any of it.

The research came later — a year spent ranking 1,500 companies for India's first supply chain index — and then Chicago, for my MS at DePaul. These days I'm as happy writing SQL as I am on the phone with a supplier. I think that mix is the whole point.

DDMRPSix Sigma Green Belt TableauPython · R · SQL AnylogistixDePaul · Kellstadt

Projects

Built, modeled,
shipped.

MGT 594 · Machine Learning
Retail Demand Forecasting
Team ML project on retail store inventory data — EDA and feature engineering across inventory levels, orders, pricing and discounts, then trained and compared models to forecast units sold and flag replenishment risk.
Pythonpandasscikit-learnseaborn
Industry Project · KeHE
Inbound Truckload RFP
Worked a real 2024 inbound TL RFP for KeHE Distributors in DePaul's transportation practicum — analyzed lane-level carrier bids and built an award recommendation balancing cost, coverage and service.
TransportationProcurementBid Analysis
Self-Directed
AI-Automated SC Analytics
End-to-end analytics stack built solo: a Supabase Postgres warehouse, Quadratic AI spreadsheets for analysis, and n8n workflow automation wiring supply chain datasets to automated Gmail alerts.
n8nPostgreSQLQuadratic AIAutomation
Machine Learning
Customer Segmentation
K-Means clustering over income, spending score and channel mix to carve a retail customer base into actionable segments — elbow-method validation and a tailored loyalty-program play per cluster.
PythonK-MeansSegmentation
Machine Learning
Churn Prediction Model
Decision-tree classifier on tenure, charges, support calls and contract type — tuned depth and leaf size, then translated the tree's splits into concrete retention actions for at-risk customers.
scikit-learnDecision TreesClassification
Decision Modeling
Optimization in Excel
Linear-programming production models (M&D Chemicals) and decision-analysis labs in Excel Solver — formulating constraints, reading sensitivity reports and running what-if scenarios on cost and capacity.
Excel SolverLinear ProgrammingSensitivity
Writing

Published thinking.

DePaul · Inside Kellstadt Blog
A Day in the Life of an MS in Supply Chain Management Student
My published piece on the Kellstadt blog — the train commute to the Loop Campus, collaborative study sessions, evening classes, and the transportation optimization model I built integrating demand, capacity uncertainty and cost-risk tradeoffs.
Student VoiceChicagoRead ↗
 ISCM · National Publication
Top 20 Supply Chain Champions of India
India’s first national supply chain index — I analyzed 1,500+ public companies across 24 sectors and helped build the ranking methodology blending financial metrics with ESG, resilience and technology factors.
R · TableauResearchVisit ↗
Field Notes · Read Here
What a Live Truckload RFP Taught Me About Carrier Pricing
The cheapest lane bid is rarely the cheapest lane. Working KeHE’s real 2024 inbound RFP data, I learned why buyers pay premiums on purpose — and what a bid sheet doesn’t tell you.
Transportation4 min readOpen ↗
Field Notes · Read Here
Ranking 1,500 Companies: Building India’s First Supply Chain Index
How do you compare a pharma cold chain to a cement dispatch network? Behind the methodology of the Top 20 Supply Chain Champions of India — and what almost broke it.
Research5 min readOpen ↗
Field Notes · Read Here
I Built an ERP Solo to Understand the Systems I’ll Manage
Most supply chain people can drive an ERP. I wanted to know what’s under the hood — so I built one with Django, Python and SQL, and it changed how I think about every system I’ll ever use.
Django · SQL4 min readOpen ↗

Scroll to explore the full journey
Operations

How I Think About Supply Chain

Keep scrolling — one truck, one continuous 3D shot. It loads at the factory, stores at the warehouse, stocks the store, and ends at a customer's doorstep. The camera rides along.

Stop 01 · Manufacture

It starts on the
factory floor

Goods come off the line and onto the truck — the sourcing and production discipline behind ₹200K in FY24 procurement savings at Kothari Electronics.

Stop 02 · Store

Inventory finds
its home

Into the warehouse, onto the racks — buffer stock, reorder points and the MRP discipline that lifted material availability 30% at Mentone Concretes.

Stop 03 · Retail

Shelves meet
demand

Distribution puts product where the forecast says it sells — the demand-planning lens I apply from SKU-level data to national indices.

Stop 04 · Deliver

The last mile ends
at a doorstep

A parcel, a porch, a promise kept — every link before it exists for this moment. That end-to-end view is how I approach supply chain.

Warehouse
Retail
Doorstep
